Title: Dispatcher signatures use na_strategy and epsilon_mode naming
Evidence: 0/16 wrappers expose formal na.rm; epsilon.type/value are absent across audited signatures.
Impact: Compatibility-sensitive callers may need argument translation rather than direct signature matching.
Recommended next action: Review whether compatibility shims or documentation should clarify argument-name translation before behavioral changes.
Likely files affected: R/gof.R; R/ggof.R; R/preproc.R; man/gof.Rd; man/ggof.Rd; man/preproc.Rd

Title: Most exported wrappers do not declare na.rm formally
Evidence: Signature matrix shows scalar wrappers rely on ... rather than a formal na.rm parameter.
Impact: Static signature checks may treat wrappers as less directly compatible even though runtime na.rm works through ...
Recommended next action: Document or normalize na.rm expectations across exported wrappers before stabilization work.
Likely files affected: R/alpha.R; R/beta.R; R/HFB.R; R/APFB.R; R/mae.R; R/NSeff.R; R/pbias.R; R/r.R; R/rNSeff.R; R/rsr.R; R/wsNSeff.R

Title: ggof returns a data.frame-based batch result
Evidence: ggof_behavior_results.txt records hydro_metrics_batch|data.frame output and no graphics-device change.
Impact: Downstream plotting-oriented expectations may not hold for ggof consumers.
Recommended next action: Clarify ggof output expectations in documentation and downstream compatibility tests.

Title: APFB rejects non-indexed inputs
Evidence: input_shape_behavior_matrix.csv records APFB numeric, matrix, and data.frame cases as unsupported with explicit indexed-input errors.
Impact: APFB cannot be used as a drop-in wrapper for plain numeric inputs.
Recommended next action: Document indexed-input requirements and verify whether wrapper callers need preflight checks.

Title: preproc rejects matrix and data.frame inputs
Evidence: input_shape_behavior_matrix.csv records preproc matrix/data.frame cases as unsupported while vector and zoo cases succeed.
Impact: preproc is less shape-flexible than several wrapper functions built on gof.
Recommended next action: Document current preproc shape limits and decide whether caller-side normalization is required.

Title: HFB fails on insufficient high-flow support points
Evidence: HFB requires at least 3 points at or above the high-flow threshold.
Impact: Short or sparse high-flow series can fail deterministically before producing a scalar result.
Recommended next action: Preserve this exact failure mode in future tests when threshold-sensitive behavior is revised.
